title: State commits $4M to address substance use crisis at Mass. and Cass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: State allocates $4 million to tackle substance use crisis around Mass. and Cass in Boston.
Largest funding portion, $2.24 million, allocated for housing up to 200 recovering individuals.
$500,000 allocated for Suffolk County DA's pre-arraignment diversion program focused on treatment.

title: Family of Stephenson King Jr., the man killed by Boston police officer now charged with manslaughter, demands transparency
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Family of Stephenson King Jr. calls for release of body-camera footage from his March shooting by Officer Nicholas O'Malley.
Officer Nicholas O'Malley charged with manslaughter for shooting King during a confrontation in a stolen car.
Attorney Ben Crump and King's family stress need for transparency, citing King's mental health issues and police accountability.

title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, from Wilmington, will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is running for South Boston Neighborhood House, honoring her late grandfather and father.
Dennett's family has deep roots in South Boston, including Joseph's Bakery, a community staple since 1967.

title: She’s running Boston for her mom and cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sophie Goad, 27, from Burbank, CA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Goad's mother was diagnosed with clear cell odontogenic carcinoma and is now cancer-free after treatment.
Goad aims to raise $15,124 for cancer research, surpassing last year's fundraising by $1, contributing to a $8.75 million team goal.

title: Jaylen Brown says underwater training with a surfing legend helped him deal with anxiety
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown used underwater training to improve mental resilience and manage anxiety after a tough 2023 playoffs.
Brown sought guidance from surfing legend Laird Hamilton on oxygen deprivation techniques for underwater drills.
The training contributed to Brown's career-year, averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists for the Celtics.

title: Police search for owner, dog after man and dog are attacked in Everett
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett police are searching for a dog owner after their pet attacked a man and another dog on Tuesday.
The injured dog, Mushroom, required 50 stitches and multiple surgeries at BluePearl veterinary hospital in Charlestown.
The dog's owner was taken to Mass General Hospital; both are expected to recover, according to police.

title: Mass. House passes bill restricting teen social media and phones in schools
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts House passed a bill banning cellphones in schools and restricting social media access for children under 16.
Children under 14 are barred from social media, while 14-15-year-olds need parental consent under the new House bill.
The legislation faces opposition from some LGBTQ groups concerned about safety and privacy implications for youths.

title: How Boston.com readers feel about the Boston Marathon not making this list of best marathons
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 77% of Boston.com readers in a poll believe the Boston Marathon should be on USA Today's best marathons list.
Some readers cite Boston Marathon's iconic history and qualification standards as reasons it should have been included.
Critics argue the marathon has become too commercial and elitist, diminishing its status as a top event.

title: Increase in bear encounters prompts new requirements for White Mountains visitors
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: White Mountain National Forest mandates bear canisters for overnight food storage in Pemigewasset Wilderness starting May 1.
Bear incidents have increased due to improperly stored food, prompting the new requirement for resistant canisters.
A free canister loan program is available at multiple locations in New Hampshire to comply with new rules.

title: Southbridge woman tried to hit teen with car while driving with daughter, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Samantha Angers, 39, charged for allegedly trying to hit a teen with her car in Southbridge.
Police report Angers pursued the teen after an altercation involving her daughter, driving erratically.
Angers faces multiple charges, with bail set at $15,000 pending her Dudley District Court arraignment.
